questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
If the average translational kinetic energy of a molecule in a gas is equal to the kinetic energy of an electron accelerating from rest through $10 \mathrm{~V}$, then the temperature of the gas molecule is
$$
\left(\text { Boltzmann constant }=1.38 \times 10^{-23} \mathrm{JK}^{-1}\right)
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: At NTP the density of a gas is 1.3 kg/m<sup>3</sup> and the velocity of sound propagation in the gas is 330 m/s. The degree of freedom of gas molecule is-
questions.solution:
V = γ P ρ

3 3 0 = γ × 1 × 1 0 5 1.3

3 3 2 × 1 0 0 × 1.3 1 × 1 0 5 = γ

1.089 × 1 0 3 × 1 0 2 × 1.3 1 × 1 0 5 = γ

2 f + 1 = γ = 1.4 = 7 5

2 f = 2 5

f = 5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A bubble is at the bottom of the lake of depth h. As the bubble comes to surface, its radius increases three times. If atmospheric pressure is equal to l metre of water column, then h is equal to
questions.solution:
From Boyle's law

pV= constant

∴              p1V1=p2V2

Here, p1=ρgh+l,  V1=43πr3

p2=ρgl,  V2=43π(3r)3

∴          h+l43πr3=l×43π3r3

or                        h+l=27l

∴                               h=26l
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the speed of sound in a mixture of 2 moles of Helium and 2 moles of Hydrogen at temperature $\frac{972}{5} \mathrm{~K}$ is $n \times 100 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$, then the value of $n$ is (Take, $\left.R=\frac{25}{3} \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}\right)$
questions.solution:
Given, $T=\frac{972}{5} \mathrm{~K}, R=\frac{25}{3} \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$

Molecular mass of the mixture
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \quad=\frac{n_{\mathrm{He}} M_{\mathrm{He}}+n_H M_H}{n_{\mathrm{He}}+n_H} \\
& \because n_{\mathrm{He}}=2 \text { moles, } n_H=2 \text { moles } \\
& M_{\mathrm{He}}=4, M_H=2
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Molecular mass of the mixtures,
$$
\begin{aligned}
M & =\frac{2 \times 4+2 \times 2}{2+2} \\
& =\frac{8+4}{4}=3 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{mole}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\because$ Degree of freedom of mixture
$$
\begin{aligned}
f=\frac{2 \times 3+2 \times 5}{2+2} & =4 \\
\because \quad f_{\text {mix }}=1+\frac{2}{f} & =1.5 \\
\because \text { Speed of sound } & =\sqrt{\frac{f_{\text {mix }} R T}{M}} \\
& =\sqrt{\frac{1.5 \times \frac{25}{3} \times \frac{972}{5}}{3 \times 10^{-3}}} \\
& =900=n \times 100 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, $n=9$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The temperature of an ideal gas is increased from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> . If the <math><mi>r</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>s</mi></math> speed of the gas molecule is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> , then at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> it becomes
questions.solution:
∵  rms speed of molecule Vrms∝T

for T=100 K

Vrms1∝100

For T=400 K

Vrms2∝400

therefore, Vrms1Vrms2=100400

or VVrms2=12

or Vrms2=2V
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A closed hollow insulated cylinder is filled with gas at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and also contains an insulated piston of negligible weight and negligible thickness at the middle point. The gas in one side of the piston is heated to $100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. If the piston moves $5 \mathrm{~cm}$. The length of the hollow cylinder is
questions.solution:
From Charle's law, $\frac{V_1}{V_2}=\frac{T_1}{T_2}$
Volume $(V)$ is proportional to the length of hollow cylinder.
$\begin{aligned} \frac{l_1}{l_2} & =\frac{T_1}{T_2} \\ \frac{l+5}{l-5} & =\frac{273+100}{273+0} \\ \frac{l+5}{l-5} & =\frac{373}{273} \\ \frac{2 l}{10} & =\frac{646}{100} \\ & =\frac{646 \times 10}{2 \times 100}=32.3 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \text { Length of cylinder } & =2 l=2 \times 32.3 \\ & =64.6 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The R.M.S. speed of oxygen molecules at temperature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> (in kelvin) is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">v</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. As the temperature becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> and the oxygen gas dissociates into atomic oxygen, then the speed of atomic oxygen
questions.solution:
v∝γ1T

v2∝2×γ2T2=2×γ2×4T       ∵T2=4T

v2v1=22γ2γ1

v2v1=25×5×23×7

v2=25021v

v2≅22v
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Under standard conditions, the density of a gas is \(\frac{1400}{1089} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{-} \mathrm{m}^{-3}\) and the speed of sound propagation in it is \(330 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}\), then the number of degrees of freedom of the gas molecules is
questions.solution:
Given, density of gas, \(\rho=\frac{1400}{1089} \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^3\) speed of sound, \(v=330 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}\) and under standard condition, Pressure of gas, \(p=1 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2\)
If \(\gamma\) be the ratio of \(C_p\) and \(C_V\) of a gas, then the speed of sound in gas is given by
\(\begin{aligned}
& \qquad v=\sqrt{\frac{\gamma P}{\rho}} \text { or } \frac{\gamma P}{\rho}=v^2 \\
& \quad \gamma=\frac{v^2 \rho}{p}=\frac{330 \times 330}{10^5} \times \frac{1400}{1089} \\
& \quad \gamma=1.4 \\
& \text { So, } \quad \gamma=\frac{C_p}{C_V}=1.4
\end{aligned} \quad\left[\begin{array}{l}
\because \gamma=1+\frac{2}{f} \\
\text { Since, for diatomic gas, the volume of }
\end{array}\right] \begin{aligned}
& \gamma \text { is 1.4. } \\
& \text { Hence, the degree of freedom for }
\end{aligned}\)
So, \(\gamma=\frac{C_p}{C_V}=1.4\)
Since, for diatomic gas, the volume of \(\left[\begin{array}{l}
\because \gamma=1+\frac{2}{f} \\
1.4=1+\frac{2}{f} \\
\therefore f=5
\end{array}\right]\)\(\gamma\) is 1.4.
Hence, the degree of freedom for
diatomic gas is equal to 5.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A diatomic gas consisting of rigid molecules is at a temperature of $87^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. If the moment of inertia of the rotating diatomic rigid molecule is $2.76 \times 10^{-39} \mathrm{gcm}^2$, then the rms angular speed of the molecule is (Boltzmann constant $=1.38 \times 10^{-23} \mathrm{JK}^{-1}$ )
questions.solution:
Moment of inertia of diatomic molecule, $$ I=\frac{2}{3} M r^2 $$
rms speed of molecule, $$ v=\sqrt{\frac{3 k T}{M}} $$ rms angular speed, $$ \begin{aligned} \omega=\frac{v}{r} & =\frac{\sqrt{3 k T / M}}{\sqrt{3 I / 2 M}}=\sqrt{\frac{2 k T}{I}} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{2 \times 1.38 \times 10^{-23} \times(87+273)}{2.76 \times 10^{-46}}} \\ & =\sqrt{360 \times 10^{23}}=\sqrt{36 \times 10^{24}} \\ & =6 \times 10^{12} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A mixture of two gases is contained in a vessel. The Gas <math><mn>1</mn></math> is monoatomic and gas <math><mn>2</mn></math> is diatomic and the ratio of their molecular masses <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math>. The ratio of root mean square speeds of the molecules of two gases is
questions.solution:
From kinetic theory of gases

P=13MV×Vrms2

Since, P and V are constant (same)

∴ M1Vrms12=M2Vrms22

⇒Vrms12Vrms22=M2M1

⇒Vrms1Vrms2=M2M1=4/1=2    given,M1M2=14
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Statement (I) : Gas thermometers are less sensitive than liquid thermometers.

Statement (II) : The ratio of universal gas constant and avagadro's number is called Boltzman's constant.

Statement (III) : The density of a given mass of a gas at constant pressure is inversely proportional to its absolute temperature.

The correct option among the following is
options: Statements I, II, III are true.
Statements I, II are true, but statement III is false.
Statements II, III are true, but statement I is false.
Statements I, II, III are false.
questions.solution:
Thermal expansion in gases is more than thermal expansion in liquids. Therefore, gas thermometers are more sensitive than liquid thermometers.

The ratio of universal gas constant and avagadro's number is called Boltzman's constant. k=RNA

At constant pressure, V∝T therefore, the density of a given mass of a gas at constant pressure is inversely proportional to its absolute temperature.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The temperature at which a  molecule of nitrogen will have the same rms velocity as a  molecule of oxygen at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>127</mn><mo> </mo><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Root mean square velocity

vrms=3RTM

where R is gas constant, T the temperature and M molecular weight.

Given, MN2=28,    MO2=32,   TO2=127 ℃=127+273=400 K

∴                vO2vN2=TO2MO2×MN2TN2=40032×28TN2=1

⇒                TN2=350 K=77 ℃.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A liquid of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>250</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> is kept warm in a vessel using an electric heater. The liquid is maintained at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>57</mn><mo> </mo><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> when the power supplied by the heater is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math> and surrounding temperature is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><mo> </mo><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. As the heater is switched off, it took <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> time for the temperature of the liquid to fall from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>47</mn><mo> </mo><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>46</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>9</mn><mo> </mo><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. The specific heat capacity of the liquid is
questions.solution:
The expression for the rate of heat flow is,

dqdt=-kT-T0

Here T0=Surrounding temperature

From first condition-|

30=-k57-27

⇒  k=-1

Using second condition-

dqdt=-kT-T0

but dqdt=msdTdt

∴  msdTdt=-k T-T0

Substitute the given values in the above equation.

2501000s47-46.910=--1 47-27

⇒  s=8000 J kg-1 K-1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A cylinder consists of a gas mixture of helium and oxygen. If the mass of helium is $4 \mathrm{~g}$ and the mass of oxygen is $32 \mathrm{~g}$, then the ratio of specific heat of the mixture $\left(C_p / C_V\right)$
questions.solution:
Mass of helium, $m_{\mathrm{He}}=4 \mathrm{~g}$
Mass of oxygen, $m_{\mathrm{O}}=32 \mathrm{~g}$
Helium is a monoatomic gas.
$\therefore$ Degrees of freedom, $f_1=3$
Oxygen is a diatomic gas.
$\therefore$ Degrees of freedom, $f_2=5$
$4 \mathrm{~g}$ helium contains $N_1=N_A=6.023 \times 10^{23}$ number of molecules
$32 \mathrm{~g}$ oxygen contains $N_2=N_A$ number of molecules.
(As atomic mass of oxygen is $16 \mathrm{~g}$ )
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \frac{C_p}{C_V} & =\gamma \\
& =\frac{N_1\left(2+f_1\right)+N_2\left(2+f_2\right)}{N_1 f_1+N_2 f_2} \\
& =\frac{N_A(2+3)+N_A(2+5)}{3 N_A+5 N_A} \\
& =\frac{5+7}{3+5}=\frac{12}{8}=\frac{3}{2}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: How many rotational degrees of freedom does a rigid diatomic molecule have?
questions.solution: A rigid diatomic molecule can store energy only by two possible ways of rotation, so it has only 2 rotational degrees of freedom.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The rms speed of hydrogen molecule at a certain temperature is <math><mi>v</mi></math>. If the temperature is doubled and hydrogen gas dissociates into atomic hydrogen, the rms speed will become
questions.solution:
Let the molar mass of H2 is M .

 Hydrogen gas is dissociated into atomic hydrogen.

 It molar mass is equal to M2 .

vrms=3RTM

v1v2=T1MO1×MO2T2

T2=2T1 and MO2=MO12=M2

⇒v1v2=T1M.M/22T1=14

v2=2v1=2v
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A thermally insulated vessel with nitrogen gas at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is moving with a velocity of $100 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. If the vessel is stopped suddenly, then the percentage change in the pressure of the gas is nearly
(assume entire loss in $\mathrm{KE}$ of the gas is given as heat to gas and $R=8.3 \mathrm{Jmol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$ )
questions.solution:
Let there are $n$ moles of $N_2$ gas in the cylinder.
Assuming all of $K, E$ appears in form of heat, $n\left(\frac{1}{2} M v^2\right)=\frac{f}{2} n R \Delta T$
Here, $M=28 \mathrm{~g}=28 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~kg}, f=5$
Increment in pressure due to change of temperature is
Also, $\Delta p=\frac{n R \Delta T}{V}$
So, $\frac{\Delta p}{p}=\frac{\left(\frac{n R \Delta T}{V}\right)}{\left(\frac{n R T}{V}\right)}=\frac{n R \Delta T}{n R T} \Rightarrow \frac{\Delta p}{p}=\frac{n M v^2}{f n R T}=\frac{M v^2}{f R T}$
So, percentage change in pressure is,
$$
\therefore \frac{\Delta p}{p} \times 100=\frac{28 \times 10^{-3} \times 100 \times 100}{5 \times 8.3 \times 300} \times 100=2.25 \%
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: We have a jar filled with gas characterized by parameters $\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{V}, \mathrm{T}$ and another jar B filled with gas having parameters $2 \mathrm{P}, \frac{\mathrm{V}}{4}, 2 \mathrm{~T}$, where symbols have their usual meaning. The ratio of number of molecules in jar A to those in jar B is
questions.solution:
According to the gas equation, $\mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{Nk}_{\mathrm{B}} \mathrm{T}$ For the first gas, we have,
$\mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{N}_1 \mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{B}} \mathrm{T}$
For the second gas, we have,
(2P) $\left(\frac{\mathrm{V}}{4}\right)=\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{B}}(2 \mathrm{~T})$
$\mathrm{PV}=4 \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{B}} \mathrm{T}$
From equations (i) and (ii)
$\mathrm{N}_1=4 \mathrm{~N}_2 \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{N}_1}{\mathrm{~N}_2}=4$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A gas mixture contains $n_1$ moles of a monoatomic gas and $n_2$ moles of gas of rigid diatomic molecules. Each molecule in monoatomic and diatomic gas has 3 and 5 degrees of freedom respectively. If the adiabatic exponent $\left(\frac{C_p}{C_V}\right)$ for this gas mixture is 1.5 , then the ratio $\frac{n_1}{n_2}$ will be
questions.solution:
For a gas mixture,
$$
C_V=\frac{n_1 C_{V_1}+n_2 C_{V_2}}{n_1+n_2} \text { and } C_p=\frac{n_1 C_{p_1}+n_2 C_{p_2}}{n_1+n_2}
$$

For monoatomic gas,
$$
n=n_1, C_{V_1}=\frac{3}{2} R, \quad C_{p_1}=\frac{5}{2} R
$$

For diatomic gas,
$$
n=n_2, C_{V_2}=\frac{5}{2} R \text { and } C_{p_2}=\frac{7}{2} R
$$

Now given for gas mixture,
$$
\begin{gathered}
\quad \frac{C_p}{C_V}=1.5 \\
\text { So, } 1.5=\frac{\frac{n_1}{n_2}\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)+\frac{7}{2}}{\frac{n_1}{n_2}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)+\frac{5}{2}} \quad \frac{9}{2}\left(\frac{n_1}{n_2}\right)+\frac{15}{2}=\left(\frac{n_1}{n_2}\right) 5+7 \\
\Rightarrow \frac{n_1}{n_2}=1
\end{gathered}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
At what temperature is the R.M.S. velocity of Hydrogen molecule equal to that of
an oxygen molecule at $47^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ ?
(Molecular weight of hydrogen $=2$, Molecular weight of oxygen $=32$ )
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a vessel, the ideal gas is at a pressure $\mathrm{P}$. If the mass of all the molecules is halved and their speed is doubled, then resultant pressure of the gas will be
questions.solution:
We know,
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{ms}}^2=\frac{3 \mathrm{PV}}{\mathrm{Nm}} \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{P}=\frac{1}{3} \frac{\mathrm{mN}}{\mathrm{V}} \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{ms}}^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{P} \propto \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{ms}}^2 \\
& \therefore \quad \frac{\mathrm{P}_2}{\mathrm{P}_1}=\frac{\mathrm{m}_2}{\mathrm{~m}_1} \times\left[\frac{\mathrm{v}_2}{\mathrm{v}_1}\right]^2 \\
&=\frac{\left(\frac{\mathrm{m}_1}{2}\right)}{\mathrm{m}_1}\left[\frac{2 \mathrm{v}_1}{\mathrm{v}_1}\right]^2 \\
& \ldots . .\left(\because \text { given } \mathrm{m}_2=\frac{\mathrm{m}_1}{2} \text { and } \mathrm{v}_2=2 \mathrm{v}_1\right)
\end{aligned}$
$\frac{\mathrm{P}_2}{\mathrm{P}_1}=2$
$\therefore \quad P_2=2 P_1$
$=2 \mathrm{P} \quad \cdots\left(\right.$ given $\left.\mathrm{P}_1=\mathrm{P}\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Tyre of a bicycle has volume $2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^3$. Initially, the tube is filled $75 \%$ of its volume by air at atmospheric pressure $10^5 \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}$. When a rider is on the bicycle, the area of contact of tyre with road is $24 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}^2$. The mass of rider with bicycle is $120 \mathrm{~kg}$. If a pump delivers a volume $500 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of air in each stroke, then the number of strokes required to inflate the tyre is $\left(g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$
questions.solution:
Pressure against which pump has to deliver air
$=$ Pressure due to weight of rider and cycle
+ Atmospheric pressure initially inside tyre
$$
=\frac{F}{A}+p_{\mathrm{atm}}=\frac{120 \times 10}{24 \times 10^{-4}}+1 \times 10^5=6 \times 10^5 \frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{m}^2}
$$
Number of moles of air in the tube
$$
n_1=\frac{\mathrm{pV}}{\mathrm{RT}}=\frac{6 \times 10^5 \times 2 \times 10^{-3}}{\mathrm{RT}}
$$
Volume of these moles at atmospheric pressure is
$$
\begin{aligned}
V_1 & =\frac{n R T}{p_{\text {atm }}}=\frac{6 \times 10^5 \times 2 \times 10^{-3}}{1 \times 10^5} \\
& =12 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^3
\end{aligned}
$$
Initial volume of air inside tyre
$$
V_0=\frac{75}{100} \times 2 \times 10^{-3}=1.5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^3
$$
So, to inflate the tube volume to be pumped in is
$$
\begin{aligned}
V_2 & =V_1-V_0=(12-1.5) \times 10^{-3} \\
& =10.5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^3
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, number of strokes of pump required
$$
=N=\frac{V_2}{V_{\text {pump }}}=\frac{10.5 \times 10^{-3}}{500 \times 10^{-6}}=21
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion: According to kinetic theory of gases, all the collision between molecule-molecule, and between molecule-walls of container are perfectly elastic, so speed of each molecule in an isolated gaseous system remains constant.

Reason: Distribution of speed among the molecules of gas does not change with time for the above system.
options: If both Assertion and Reason are true and the Reason is correct explanation of the Assertion.
If both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is not correct explanation of the Assertion.
If Assertion is true but the Reason is false.
If Assertion is false but Reason is true.
questions.solution:
The molecules of the gas or liquid are moving randomly, they are getting collide, in each collision, the momentum is getting a transfer from one molecule to another molecule, so the total energy of the system is constant but the molecular energy of the molecules is not constant, hence their velocity is also not a constant value.

From the given graph, we can conclude that the ratio of the number of the molecules with certain velocity to the total number of molecules is constant. It is the cause of the conservation of energy of the system.

 

Speed of a molecule can change.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For an ideal gas the density of the gas is $\rho_0$ when temperature and pressure of the gas are $T_0$ and $\mathrm{P}_0$ respectively. When the temperature of the gas is $2 \mathrm{~T}_0$, its pressure will be $3 \mathrm{P}_0$. The new density will be
questions.solution:
Density $\propto \mathrm{P} / \mathrm{T}$
So, $\frac{\mathrm{d}_2}{\mathrm{~d}_1}=\frac{\mathrm{P}_2}{\mathrm{~T}_2} \times \frac{\mathrm{T}_1}{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{t}}}$
$\therefore \quad$ The new density is:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{d}_2=\rho_0 \times \frac{3 \mathrm{P}_0}{2 \mathrm{~T}_0} \times \frac{\mathrm{T}_0}{\mathrm{P}_0} \\
& \mathrm{~d}_2=\frac{3}{2} \rho_0
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The rms speed of oxygen molecule at a certain temperature is $600 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. If the temperature is doubled and oxygen molecule dissociates into atomic oxygen atoms, the new rms speed is
questions.solution:
$v_{\mathrm{rms}}=\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}}$ Given, at temperature $T_1$,
At temperature, $T_2=2 \pi T_1$, due to dissociation
Dividing Eq. (i) by Eq. (ii), we get $$ \Rightarrow \quad \frac{v}{600}=2 \quad \Rightarrow v=1200 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A piece of metal weighing $100 \mathrm{~g}$ is heated to $80^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and dropped into $1 \mathrm{~kg}$ of cold water in an insulated container at $15^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. If the final temperature of the water in the container is $15.69^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, the specific heat of the metal in $\mathrm{J} / \mathrm{g}^{\circ} \cdot \mathrm{C}$ is:
questions.solution:
Heat exchanged is given by, $\mathrm{Q}=\mathrm{m} \times \mathbf{s} \times\left(\mathrm{T}_2-\mathrm{T}_1\right)$ where $\mathbf{s}=$ specific heat
So, when heated metal is dropped into the cold water, we can write:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{m}_{\text {metal }} \times \mathrm{s}_{\text {metal }} \times\left(\mathrm{T}_2-\mathrm{T}_1\right)_{\text {metal }}=\mathrm{m}_{\text {water }} \times \mathrm{s}_{\text {water }} \times\left(\mathrm{T}_2-\mathrm{T}_1\right)_{\text {water }} \\
& 0.1 \times \mathrm{s}_{\text {metal }} \times(80-15.69)=1 \times 4.18 \times(15.69-15) \\
& \mathrm{s}_{\text {metal }}=0.45 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{g}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In an ideal gas, if the masses of all molecules are doubled and their speeds are halved, then the ratio of initial and final pressures of the gas is
questions.solution:
Pressure of an ideal gas is given by
$p=\frac{1}{3} \frac{m N}{V}\left(\mathbf{v}^2\right)$
When mass of molecule is doubled and speed is halved, pressure becomes
$p^{\prime}=\frac{1}{3} \frac{(2 m) N}{V} \cdot\left(\frac{\mathbf{v}}{2}\right)^2=\frac{1}{2}\left\{\frac{1}{3} \frac{m N}{V}(\mathbf{v})^2\right\}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2} p^{\prime} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{p}{p^{\prime}}=\frac{2}{1} \Rightarrow p: p^{\prime}=2: 1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Volume-temperature graph at atmospheric pressure for a monoatomic gas (<math><mi>V</mi><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>T</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> in </mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">celcius</mi></math>) is
questions.solution:
PV=nRt V= nR P ( T c +273.15) V=( nR P ) T c + 273.15nR P
Positive slope positive intercept

The volume of gas is zero at absolute zero temperature ( 0 K )
Thus, the volume has a positive value at 0°C since the volume is directly proportonal to the absolute temperature and 0  K=−273.15°C
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: One mole of a monatomic ideal gas undergoes the process $\mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{B}$ in the given $p$ - $V$ diagram. Specific heat capacity in the process is
questions.solution:
According to the question,
Now, the temperature at point $A$ and $B$ By, $p V=n R T$ (from ideal gas equation) Temperature at point $A$, $$ T_A=\frac{3 p_0 V_0}{R} $$ Temperature at point $B$, $$ T_B=\frac{30 p_0 V_0}{R} $$ So, the temperature difference, $\Delta T=T_B-T_A$ $$ \begin{array}{ll} \therefore & \Delta T=\frac{30 p_0 V_0}{R}-\frac{3 p_0 V_0}{R} \\ \text { or, } & \Delta T=\frac{27 p_0 V_0}{R} \end{array} $$ or, Now, due to the adiabatic process, change in internal energy, $$ \therefore \quad \Delta U=n C_v \Delta T $$
Work done by the gas undergoes the process $$ \begin{aligned} & A \rightarrow \mathrm{B} . \\ & \therefore W=\text { Area under } p \text { - } V \text { graph } \\ & \text { or } \quad W=18 p_0 V_0 \end{aligned} $$
Now, by first law of thermodynamics, $$ Q=\Delta U+W $$ By putting the values from Eqs. (i) and (ii) to above Eq. we get $$ \begin{array}{lcl} \therefore & Q=\frac{3}{2} R \Delta T+\frac{2}{3} R \Delta T \\ \text { or } & C \Delta T=\frac{13}{6} R \Delta T \\ & (\because Q=n C \Delta T, \text { where } n=1 \text { mole }) \\ \text { or } & C=\frac{13}{6} R \end{array} $$ So, the specific heat capacity in the process $A \rightarrow B$ is $C=\frac{13}{6} R$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: At a temperature of $314 \mathrm{~K}$ and a pressure of $100 \mathrm{kPa}$, the speed of sound in a gas is $1380 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. The radius of each gas molecule is $0.5 Å$. The frequency of sound at which the wavelength of sound wave in the gas becomes equal to the mean free path of the gas molecules is $\left(\right.$ Boltzmann constant $=1.38 \times 10^{-23} \mathrm{JK}^{-1}$.)
questions.solution:
Given, temperature, $T=314 \mathrm{~K}$, pressure, $p=100 \mathrm{kPa},=1.0 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~Pa}$, speed of sound, $v=1380 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ and diameter of gas molecule, $d=10^{-10} \mathrm{~m}$ or radius $r=\frac{1}{2} \times 10^{-10} \mathrm{~m}=\frac{1}{2} Å$
So, from Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get $$ v=\frac{\sqrt{2} \pi d^2 p \times v}{k T} $$ Now, putting the given values, we get $$ \begin{aligned} & =\frac{\sqrt{2} \times 3.14 \times 10^{-20} \times 10^5 \times 1380}{1.38 \times 10^{-23} \times 314} \\ v & =\sqrt{2} \times 10^9 \mathrm{~Hz} \Rightarrow v=1000 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{MHz} \end{aligned} $$ Hence, the correct option is (b).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The average translational kinetic energy of $N$ molecules in a gas is $E_1$. The kinetic energy of the electron $(e)$ accelerated from rest through potential difference $V$ volt is $E_2$. The temperature at which $E_1=E_2$ possible is ( $R=$ gas constant, $N=$ number of molecules)
questions.solution:
The average translational KE of a gas molecule is $\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{R}{N}\right) T=E_1$
The KE of an electron accelerated from rest through a potential difference $V$ is given by,
$e V=E_2$
If $E_1=E_2$
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{R}{N}\right) T^{\prime}=E_2 \\
& \Rightarrow T^{\prime}=\frac{2 N E_2}{3 R}=\frac{2 N V e}{3 R}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: RMS velocity of oxygen molecules at NTP is $0.5 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{s}$. The RMS velocity for the hydrogen molecule at NTP is
questions.solution:
RMS velocity of molecules at NTP
$$
v_{\mathrm{rms}}=\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}}
$$
RMS velocity of oxygen molecules at NTP
$$
\left(v_1\right)=\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M_1}}=0.5 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{s}
$$

RMS velocity of hydrogen molecules at NTP
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left(v_2\right)=\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M_2}} \\
& \therefore \quad \frac{v_1}{v_2}=\sqrt{\frac{M_2}{M_1}} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{0.5}{v_2}=\sqrt{\frac{2}{32}} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{0.5}{v_2}=\frac{1}{4} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad v_2=2 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{s} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For a molecule of an ideal gas, the number density is $2 \sqrt{2} \times 10^8 \mathrm{~cm}^{-3}$ and the mean free path is $\frac{10^{-2}}{\pi} \mathrm{cm}$. The diameter of the gas molecule is
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Uranium has two isotopes of masses 235 and 238 wits. If both of them are present in wranium hexafluoride gas, find the percentage ratio of difference in rms velocities of two isotopes to the rms velocity of heavier isotope.
questions.solution:
Given,
Mass of first uranium isotope $=238$ units
Mass of second uranium isotope $=235$ units
We know that,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& v_{\text {rms }} & =\sqrt{\frac{\gamma R T}{M}} \\
\Rightarrow & v_{\text {rms }} & \propto \frac{1}{\sqrt{M}}
\end{aligned}
$$
So, the percentage ratio of difference in rms velocity of isotopes is given by
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{V_{\mathrm{rms}_2}-V_{\mathrm{rms}_1}}{V_{\mathrm{rms}_2}} \times 100 \\
& =\frac{\sqrt{M_1}-\sqrt{M_2}}{\sqrt{M_1}} \times 100 \\
& =\frac{\sqrt{238}-\sqrt{235}}{\sqrt{238}} \times 100
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{15.427-15.329}{15.427} \times 100=\frac{0.097}{15.427} \times 100 \\
& =6.34 \times 10^{-3} \times 100=0.634 \approx 0.64
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the average kinetic energy of a molecule of hydrogen gas at <math><mn>300</mn><mi> </mi><mn>K</mn></math> is <math><mi>E</mi></math>, then the average kinetic energy of a molecule of nitrogen gas at the same temperature is
questions.solution:
We know that,

Kinetic energy ∝T

In the question, temperature is same in both conditions. So, the kinetic energy in the second condition is E .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The molecular mass of a gas having r.m.s. speed four times as that of another gas having molecular mass 32 is
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{ll} & \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{rms}} \propto \frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{M}}} \\ \therefore \quad & \frac{\left(\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{rms}}\right)_2}{\left(\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{rms}}\right)_1}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{M}_1}{\mathrm{M}_2}} \\ & \text { Given that }\left(\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{rms}}\right)_2=4\left(\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{rms}}\right)_1 \\ \therefore \quad & \sqrt{\frac{32}{\mathrm{M}_2}}=4 \\ \therefore \quad & \frac{32}{\mathrm{M}_2}=16 \\ \therefore \quad & \mathrm{M}_2=2\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider an ideal gas at pressure $p$, volume $V$ and temperature $T$. The mean free path for molecules of the gas is $L$. If the radius of gas molecules, as well as pressure, volume and temperature of the gas are doubled, then the mean free path will be
questions.solution:
Given, initial values of pressure, volume,
temperature, radius of molecule of gas and mean free path are $P p, V, T, r$ and $L$, respectively.
$\therefore$ Mean free path,
$\begin{aligned} \lambda & =\frac{2 \sqrt{2} R T}{\pi r^2 N p} \\ \therefore \quad L & =\frac{2 \sqrt{2} R T}{\pi r^2 N p}\end{aligned}$
According to question if the radius of gas molecules, as well as pressure, volume and temperature of gas are doubled then
$\begin{aligned} \lambda^{\prime} & =\frac{2 \sqrt{2} R \cdot 2 T}{\pi \times 4 r^2 \times N \times 2 p} \\ & =\frac{1}{4} \cdot \frac{2 \sqrt{2} R T}{\pi r^2 N p}=\frac{L}{4}\end{aligned}$
(From Eq. (i))
Hence, the mean free path will be $\frac{L}{4}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A flask contains argon and chlorine in the ratio of $2: 1$ by mass. The temperature of the mixture is $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The ratio of average kinetic energies of two gases per molecule is
questions.solution:
(a) The average kinetic energy of molecules is given by
$\mathrm{KE}_{\mathrm{av}}=\frac{3}{2} K T$. So it depends only on temperature
$\mathrm{KE}_1: \mathrm{KE}_2=1: 1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A sample of oxygen gas and a sample of hydrogen gas both have the same mass, same volume and the same pressure. The ratio of their absolute temperature is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{P}_1 \mathrm{~V}_1=\frac{\mathrm{m}_1}{\mathrm{M}_1} \mathrm{RT}_1 \ldots$...(i)
$\mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{~V}_2=\frac{\mathrm{m}_2}{\mathrm{M}_2} \mathrm{RT}_2 \ldots$ (ii)
Dividing (i) by (ii)
$\frac{\mathrm{T}_1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}=\frac{\mathrm{M}_1}{\mathrm{M}_2}=\frac{32}{2}=\frac{16}{1}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A gas volume \(251 \mathrm{~cm}^3\) at \(20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) and pressure \(78 \mathrm{~cm}\) of \(\mathrm{Hg}\). Find its volume at NTP
questions.solution:
Volume of gas, \(V=251 \mathrm{~cm}^3\)
Temperature,
\(\begin{aligned}
& T_1=20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}=273+20=293^{\circ} \mathrm{K} \\
& p_1=78 \mathrm{~cm} \text { of } \mathrm{Hg}
\end{aligned}\)
At NTP,
Temperature, \(T_2=300 \mathrm{~K}\)
Pressure,
\(\begin{aligned}
& p_2=76 \mathrm{~cm} \text { of } \mathrm{Hg} \\
& V_2=?
\end{aligned}\)
According to ideal gas equation,
\(\begin{aligned}
& \frac{p_1 V_1}{T_1}=\frac{p_2 V_2}{T_2} \\
\Rightarrow & V_2=\frac{p_1 V_1 T_2}{p_2 T_1}=\frac{78 \times 251 \times 300}{76 \times 293} \\
& =263.8 \mathrm{~cm}^3
\end{aligned}\)
No option is matched.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
